Richard Warren
(about 1501 – 28 May 1557)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Richard Warren son of Sir John and Margaret De Leigh Warren married about 1526 at Bassingbourne, Alice Olivia Jenawaye of Bassingboun, Cambridgeshire, England.

zh.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller於大約1501年出生在英國坎布利其爾巴辛波。他於1526年與Alice Jenawaye在英國坎布利其爾巴辛波結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1557年5月2日在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾巴辛波享年57歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾坎布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller于大约1501年出生在英国坎布利其爾巴辛波。他于1526年与Alice Jenawaye在英国坎布利其爾巴辛波结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1557年5月2日在英国英格兰坎布利其爾巴辛波享年57岁，埋葬在英国英格兰坎布利其爾坎布利其。

nameMeanings.name: Warren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Warin, Werin, a borrowing of ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare Waring .

English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from La Varrenne in Seine-Maritime, France, named with a Gaulish element probably descriptive of alluvial land or sandy soil. This was the name of a major Norman family after the Conquest. In Ireland, this name has been Gaelicized as Bharain.
Irish: adopted as an English form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane , Warner ).

variants.name: Warrens
meanings: English: variant of Warren with genitival or excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Warrener
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French warrennier ‘warrener’, an officer employed to watch over game in a park or game preserve. Compare Warren .

variants.name: Murnane
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin, a shortened and dissimilated form of Ó Manannáin ‘descendant of Manannán’, name borne in Celtic mythology by a sea god associated with the Isle of Man. All three syllables appear in the variant Marrinan. This name has also been Anglicized as Warren and Warner .

variants.name: Waren
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Warren  .

variants.name: Warne
meanings: shortened form of   Warren  .
occasionally a habitational name from Wearne in Somerset. The place takes its name from a lost stream-name based on British verno- ‘alder, swamp’.
English:

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: alias
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Luis, Juan, Manuel, Jorge, Jesus, Arturo, Ramon, Pedro, Francisco, Miguel.
Spanish: from the medieval personal name Arias, of disputed origin.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

nameMeanings.name: Waller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English waler, waliere, walour, waller ‘builder of walls, mason’.
English: in Sussex, perhaps a topographic name for someone who lived by a prominent wall.

English: topographic name for someone who lived by a spring, stream, or man-made well, from Middle English waller, a derivative of Middle English walle, Old English wælle, wælle. It is a West Midlands dialect form of Weller .

variants.name: Wallar
meanings: English: variant of   Waller  .

variants.name: Weller
meanings: English (Surrey, Sussex, and Kent): topographic name for someone who lived by a spring, stream, or man-made well, from Middle English weller, willer, an agent derivative of Middle English welle (in southern England) and wille (in south western England), from Old English wella, well(e), wiella, wielle, willa, wyll(a), wylle. This type of name, a topographic term + -er, is particularly typical of Sussex and its neigbouring counties.
English: occupational name for someone who casts metal or who boils salt, from Middle English weller, willer, a derivative with -er of Old English wellan, wiellan ‘to boil’. Compare 5 below.
North German: variant of   Welle  , cognate with 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller was born about 1501, in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England. He married Alice Jenawaye in 1526, in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 2 May 1557, in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 57, and was buried in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller est né environ 1501, à Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Alice Jenawaye en 1526, à Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 2 mai 1557, à Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller wurde um 1501 in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England geboren. Er hat 1526 in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England Alice Jenawaye geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 2. Mai 1557 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller è nato il circa 1501 nel Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England. Lui sposato Alice Jenawaye nel 1526, nel Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 2 maggio 1557, nel Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Wallerは約1501年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアバシングボウルンで生まれた。1526年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアバシングボウルンでAlice Jenawayeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1557年5月2日にイギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアバシングボウルンで57歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアカンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller(은)는 대략 1501년 영국 캄브리지실르 배징보우른에서 태어났다. 그는 1526년 영국 캄브리지실르 배징보우른에서 Alice Jenawaye(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1557년 5월 2일 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 배징보우른에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller nasceu aproximadamente 1501, em Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Alice Jenawaye em 1526, em Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 2 de maio de 1557, em Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller родился около 1501 г. в местности бассингбурн, камбриджешир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Jenawaye в 1526 г. в местности бассингбурн, камбриджешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 2 мая 1557 г. в местности бассингбурн, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Richard Warren alias Waller nació aproximadamente 1501, en Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Alice Jenawaye en 1526, en Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 2 de mayo de 1557, en Bassingbourn, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
